---
title: "Django"
subtitle: "The Web Framework for Perfectionists with Deadlines"
author: "Python with Django"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [dark, custom.scss]
    highlight-style: monokai
    filters:
      - code-fullscreen
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: false
    footer: "Django — Complete Guide"
    progress: true
    code-fold: false
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    incremental: false
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.05
    center: false
  pptx:
    mermaid-format: png
mermaid:
  theme: dark

execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
---

## What is Django? {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Django** is a high-level Python web framework that encourages **rapid development** and **clean, pragmatic design**.

<br>

::: {.fragment}
**Core Philosophy**

- **DRY** — Don't Repeat Yourself
- **Convention over Configuration**
- **Batteries Included** — everything you need is built in
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Released in 2005** by Adrian Holovaty and Simon Willison  
Named after jazz guitarist **Django Reinhardt**  
Maintained by the **Django Software Foundation (DSF)**
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Used by:** Instagram, Pinterest, Disqus, Mozilla, Bitbucket, NASA
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
mindmap
  root((Django))
    ORM
      Models
      Migrations
      QuerySets
    Web Layer
      URLs
      Views
      Templates
    Features
      Admin Panel
      Auth System
      Forms
      Middleware
    Ecosystem
      DRF
      Channels
      Celery
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Django vs Other Frameworks {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

| Feature | Django | Flask | FastAPI | Express (Node) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Type | Full-stack | Micro | Async API | Micro |
| ORM | Built-in | 3rd-party | 3rd-party | 3rd-party |
| Admin Panel | ✅ Built-in | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Auth System | ✅ Built-in | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Learning Curve | Medium | Low | Low | Low |
| Performance | Good | Good | Excellent | Excellent |
| Best For | Full web apps | Simple APIs | Async APIs | JS ecosystem |

::: {.fragment}
> **Choose Django** when you need a complete, production-ready framework with admin, auth, ORM, and migrations out-of-the-box.
:::

---

## MVT Architecture {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

Django follows the **Model-View-Template (MVT)** pattern — a variant of MVC.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'flowchart': {'curve': 'orthogonal'}}}%%
graph TD
    Browser["🌐 Browser / Client"] -->|HTTP Request| URL
    URL["🔀 URL Dispatcher<br/>(urls.py)"] -->|routes to| View
    View["⚙️ View<br/>(views.py)"] -->|queries| Model
    Model["🗄️ Model<br/>(models.py)"] -->|data| DB[(Database)]
    DB -->|rows| Model
    Model -->|queryset| View
    View -->|context| Template
    Template["📄 Template<br/>(.html)"] -->|rendered HTML| Browser

    classDef browser fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef url     fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef view    fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef model   fill:#16213e,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef db      fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#aaa,color:#ccc
    classDef tpl     fill:#0a2e1a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class Browser browser
    class URL url
    class View view
    class Model model
    class DB db
    class Template tpl
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
**Model** — Defines data structure & database schema

**View** — Business logic; takes a request, returns a response

**Template** — HTML with Django Template Language (DTL)

**URL Dispatcher** — Maps URL patterns to view functions
:::

::: {.fragment}
> Unlike MVC, Django's "View" is the Controller, and the "Template" is the View.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Request–Response Lifecycle {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'sequence': {'mirrorActors': false}}}%%
sequenceDiagram
    participant C  as 🌐 Client
    participant MW as 🛡️ Middleware Stack
    participant U  as 🔀 URL Dispatcher
    participant V  as ⚙️ View
    participant M  as 🗄️ Model / ORM
    participant DB as 💾 Database
    participant T  as 📄 Template Engine

    C  ->> MW : HTTP Request
    MW ->> U  : process_request()
    U  ->> V  : route match → call view(request)
    V  ->> M  : Model.objects.filter(...)
    M  ->> DB : SQL Query
    DB -->> M : Result rows
    M  -->> V : QuerySet
    V  ->> T  : render(request, template, context)
    T  -->> V : HTML string
    V  -->> MW: HttpResponse
    MW -->> C : process_response() → Final HTTP Response
```

---

## Project Structure {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Create a project**
```bash
django-admin startproject mysite
cd mysite
python manage.py startapp blog
```

::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    ROOT["📁 mysite/"] --> MANAGE["manage.py"]
    ROOT --> PROJ["📁 mysite/ (config)"]
    ROOT --> APP["📁 blog/ (app)"]

    PROJ --> SETTINGS["settings.py"]
    PROJ --> URLS["urls.py"]
    PROJ --> WSGI["wsgi.py"]
    PROJ --> ASGI["asgi.py"]

    APP --> MODELS["models.py"]
    APP --> VIEWS["views.py"]
    APP --> APPURLS["urls.py"]
    APP --> ADMIN["admin.py"]
    APP --> FORMS["forms.py"]
    APP --> TESTS["tests.py"]
    APP --> MIGRATIONS["📁 migrations/"]
    APP --> TEMPLATES["📁 templates/"]
    APP --> STATIC["📁 static/"]

    classDef file  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef dir   fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#533483,color:#ccc
    class MANAGE,SETTINGS,URLS,WSGI,ASGI,MODELS,VIEWS,APPURLS,ADMIN,FORMS,TESTS file
    class ROOT,PROJ,APP,MIGRATIONS,TEMPLATES,STATIC dir
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
**Key files explained:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `manage.py` | CLI tool for Django commands |
| `settings.py` | All project configuration |
| `urls.py` (project) | Root URL configuration |
| `models.py` | Database schema |
| `views.py` | Request handlers |
| `admin.py` | Admin site registration |
| `migrations/` | DB schema version control |
:::
:::
:::

---

## Settings Configuration {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# mysite/settings.py

SECRET_KEY = 'your-secret-key-here'
DEBUG = True          # ⚠️ False in production
ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['*']

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    'django.contrib.admin',
    'django.contrib.auth',
    'django.contrib.contenttypes',
    'django.contrib.sessions',
    'django.contrib.messages',
    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
    'blog',           # ← your app
]

DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
        'NAME': BASE_DIR / 'db.sqlite3',
    }
}

TEMPLATES = [{
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
    'DIRS': [BASE_DIR / 'templates'],
    'APP_DIRS': True,
}]

STATIC_URL  = '/static/'
MEDIA_URL   = '/media/'
MEDIA_ROOT  = BASE_DIR / 'media'
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    SETTINGS["settings.py"] --> APPS["INSTALLED_APPS<br/>(what's active)"]
    SETTINGS --> DB["DATABASES<br/>(engine + name)"]
    SETTINGS --> TMPL["TEMPLATES<br/>(dirs + loaders)"]
    SETTINGS --> MW["MIDDLEWARE<br/>(processing chain)"]
    SETTINGS --> STATIC["STATIC_URL<br/>MEDIA_ROOT"]
    SETTINGS --> AUTH["AUTH_USER_MODEL<br/>LOGIN_URL"]
    SETTINGS --> SEC["SECRET_KEY<br/>ALLOWED_HOSTS<br/>DEBUG"]

    classDef node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    class APPS,DB,TMPL,MW,STATIC,AUTH,SEC node
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## URL Routing {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Project-level `urls.py`**
```python
from django.contrib import admin
from django.urls import path, include

urlpatterns = [
    path('admin/', admin.site.urls),
    path('blog/', include('blog.urls')),
    path('api/', include('api.urls')),
]
```

::: {.fragment}
**App-level `blog/urls.py`**
```python
from django.urls import path
from . import views

app_name = 'blog'  # namespace

urlpatterns = [
    path('',             views.index,       name='index'),
    path('<int:pk>/',    views.detail,      name='detail'),
    path('new/',         views.create,      name='create'),
    path('<int:pk>/edit/', views.update,    name='update'),
    path('<slug:slug>/', views.by_slug,     name='by-slug'),
]
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    REQ["Request: /blog/42/"] --> PROJ["project urls.py"]
    PROJ -->|"blog/ → include"| APP["blog/urls.py"]
    APP  -->|"&lt;int:pk&gt;/ → detail"| VIEW["views.detail(request, pk=42)"]

    style REQ  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    style PROJ fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    style APP  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    style VIEW fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Path converters:**  
`<int:pk>` — integer  
`<str:name>` — non-empty string  
`<slug:slug>` — slug  
`<uuid:id>` — UUID  
`<path:subpath>` — includes `/`
:::
:::
:::

---

## Function-Based Views (FBV) {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from django.shortcuts import render, get_object_or_404
from django.http import HttpResponse, HttpResponseRedirect
from django.urls import reverse
from .models import Post
from .forms import PostForm

def index(request):
    posts = Post.objects.all().order_by('-created_at')
    return render(request, 'blog/index.html', {'posts': posts})

def detail(request, pk):
    post = get_object_or_404(Post, pk=pk)
    return render(request, 'blog/detail.html', {'post': post})

def create(request):
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form = PostForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            post = form.save(commit=False)
            post.author = request.user
            post.save()
            return HttpResponseRedirect(
                reverse('blog:detail', args=[post.pk])
            )
    else:
        form = PostForm()
    return render(request, 'blog/form.html', {'form': form})
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
flowchart TD
    REQ["request arrives"] --> METHOD{request.method?}
    METHOD -->|GET| BUILD["build empty form"]
    METHOD -->|POST| BIND["bind form to POST data"]
    BIND --> VALID{form.is_valid?}
    VALID -->|Yes| SAVE["save to DB"]
    SAVE --> REDIRECT["redirect to detail"]
    VALID -->|No| RENDER_ERR["render form<br/>with errors"]
    BUILD --> RENDER["render blank form"]

    classDef decision fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef action   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef term     fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class METHOD,VALID decision
    class BUILD,BIND,SAVE,RENDER_ERR,RENDER action
    class REQ,REDIRECT term
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Class-Based Views (CBV) {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from django.views.generic import (
    ListView, DetailView,
    CreateView, UpdateView, DeleteView
)
from django.urls import reverse_lazy
from .models import Post

class PostListView(ListView):
    model         = Post
    template_name = 'blog/index.html'
    context_object_name = 'posts'
    ordering      = ['-created_at']
    paginate_by   = 10

class PostDetailView(DetailView):
    model         = Post
    template_name = 'blog/detail.html'

class PostCreateView(CreateView):
    model         = Post
    fields        = ['title', 'content', 'category']
    template_name = 'blog/form.html'
    success_url   = reverse_lazy('blog:index')

    def form_valid(self, form):
        form.instance.author = self.request.user
        return super().form_valid(form)

class PostDeleteView(DeleteView):
    model       = Post
    success_url = reverse_lazy('blog:index')
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    VIEW["django.views.generic.View"] --> BASE["Base"]
    BASE --> TEMPLATE["TemplateView"]
    BASE --> REDIRECT["RedirectView"]
    VIEW --> DETAIL["DetailView"]
    VIEW --> LIST["ListView"]
    VIEW --> EDIT["Editing Views"]
    EDIT --> CREATE["CreateView"]
    EDIT --> UPDATE["UpdateView"]
    EDIT --> DELETE["DeleteView"]
    EDIT --> FORM["FormView"]
    VIEW --> DATE["Date-based Views"]
    DATE --> ARCHIVE["ArchiveIndexView"]
    DATE --> YEAR["YearArchiveView"]
    DATE --> TODAY["TodayArchiveView"]

    classDef root   fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef group  fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef leaf   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class VIEW root
    class BASE,EDIT,DATE group
    class TEMPLATE,REDIRECT,DETAIL,LIST,CREATE,UPDATE,DELETE,FORM,ARCHIVE,YEAR,TODAY leaf
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Models & ORM {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from django.db import models
from django.contrib.auth.models import User

class Category(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True)
    slug = models.SlugField(unique=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.name

    class Meta:
        verbose_name_plural = "categories"
        ordering = ['name']

class Post(models.Model):
    STATUS = [('draft', 'Draft'), ('published', 'Published')]

    title     = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    slug      = models.SlugField(unique=True)
    author    = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE,
                                  related_name='posts')
    category  = models.ForeignKey(Category, on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
                                  null=True)
    tags      = models.ManyToManyField('Tag', blank=True)
    content   = models.TextField()
    status    = models.CharField(max_length=10, choices=STATUS,
                                  default='draft')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
erDiagram
    USER {
        int id PK
        string username
        string email
        string password
    }
    CATEGORY {
        int id PK
        string name
        string slug
    }
    POST {
        int id PK
        string title
        string slug
        string content
        string status
        datetime created_at
        datetime updated_at
        int author_id FK
        int category_id FK
    }
    TAG {
        int id PK
        string name
    }
    POST_TAGS {
        int post_id FK
        int tag_id FK
    }
    USER     ||--o{ POST : "writes"
    CATEGORY ||--o{ POST : "contains"
    POST     ||--o{ POST_TAGS : "has"
    TAG      ||--o{ POST_TAGS : "labels"
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Field Types {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Text & Numbers**

| Field | Use case |
|---|---|
| `CharField` | Short strings (max_length required) |
| `TextField` | Long text / paragraphs |
| `IntegerField` | Whole numbers |
| `FloatField` | Floating-point |
| `DecimalField` | Precise decimals (finance) |
| `BooleanField` | True / False |
| `SlugField` | URL-friendly string |
| `EmailField` | Validated email |
| `URLField` | Validated URL |

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Date, Files & Relations**

| Field | Use case |
|---|---|
| `DateField` | Date only |
| `DateTimeField` | Date + time |
| `TimeField` | Time only |
| `ImageField` | Image upload |
| `FileField` | Any file upload |
| `ForeignKey` | Many-to-one |
| `ManyToManyField` | Many-to-many |
| `OneToOneField` | One-to-one |
| `JSONField` | JSON data |

::: {.fragment}
> All fields accept `null=True`, `blank=True`, `default=`, `verbose_name=`, `help_text=`
:::
:::
:::

---

## ORM QuerySets {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# CREATE
post = Post.objects.create(title="Hello", content="World")
# or
post = Post(title="Hello"); post.save()

# READ — lazy evaluation
Post.objects.all()                        # all rows
Post.objects.filter(status='published')   # WHERE status='published'
Post.objects.exclude(status='draft')      # WHERE NOT status='draft'
Post.objects.get(pk=1)                    # single (raises if missing)
Post.objects.order_by('-created_at')      # ORDER BY DESC
Post.objects.select_related('author')     # JOIN (FK)
Post.objects.prefetch_related('tags')     # prefetch M2M

# FILTER lookups
Post.objects.filter(title__contains='Django')
Post.objects.filter(created_at__year=2025)
Post.objects.filter(author__username='alice')

# UPDATE
Post.objects.filter(status='draft').update(status='published')

# DELETE
Post.objects.filter(status='archived').delete()

# AGGREGATE
from django.db.models import Count, Avg
Post.objects.aggregate(total=Count('id'))
Post.objects.values('category').annotate(n=Count('id'))
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
flowchart LR
    MGR["Post.objects<br/>(Manager)"] --> QS["QuerySet<br/>(lazy)"]
    QS --> FILTER["filter()"]
    QS --> EXCLUDE["exclude()"]
    QS --> ORDER["order_by()"]
    QS --> VALUES["values() / values_list()"]
    QS --> ANNOTATE["annotate()"]
    QS --> AGGREGATE["aggregate() → dict"]
    QS --> SELECT["select_related()<br/>prefetch_related()"]
    FILTER --> EVAL["Evaluation<br/>(iteration, slice,<br/>len, bool, get)"]
    EXCLUDE --> EVAL
    ORDER  --> EVAL
    VALUES --> EVAL
    EVAL --> SQL["SQL Query → DB"]

    classDef mgr   fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef qs    fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef op    fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef term  fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class MGR mgr
    class QS qs
    class FILTER,EXCLUDE,ORDER,VALUES,ANNOTATE,AGGREGATE,SELECT op
    class EVAL,SQL term
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Migrations {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
Migrations are Django's way of **propagating model changes** to the database schema.

```bash
# 1. Create migration files from model changes
python manage.py makemigrations

# 2. Apply pending migrations to DB
python manage.py migrate

# 3. Show migration status
python manage.py showmigrations

# 4. Inspect SQL for a migration
python manage.py sqlmigrate blog 0001

# 5. Roll back to a specific migration
python manage.py migrate blog 0002

# 6. Squash migrations
python manage.py squashmigrations blog 0001 0005
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
flowchart TD
    A["Edit<br/>models.py"] --> B["makemigrations<br/>→ 0001_initial.py"]
    B --> C["migrate<br/>→ CREATE TABLE"]
    C --> D["Edit model<br/>(add field)"]
    D --> E["makemigrations<br/>→ 0002_add_field.py"]
    E --> F["migrate<br/>→ ALTER TABLE"]

    classDef edit  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef cmd   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef db    fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class A,D edit
    class B,E cmd
    class C,F db
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Dependency graph of migrations:**
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    AUTH["auth 0001"] --> AUTH2["auth 0002"]
    BLOG1["blog 0001"] -->|depends on| AUTH["auth 0001"]
    BLOG1 --> BLOG2["blog 0002"]
    BLOG2 --> BLOG3["blog 0003"]

    classDef auth fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef blog fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    class AUTH,AUTH2 auth
    class BLOG1,BLOG2,BLOG3 blog
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Templates & DTL {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**`templates/blog/base.html`**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <title>{% block title %}My Blog{% endblock %}</title>
  {% load static %}
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="{% static 'css/style.css' %}">
</head>
<body>
  <nav>{% include 'partials/_nav.html' %}</nav>

  <main>{% block content %}{% endblock %}</main>

  <footer>{% block footer %}© 2025{% endblock %}</footer>
</body>
</html>
```

::: {.fragment}
**`templates/blog/index.html`**
```html
{% extends 'blog/base.html' %}

{% block title %}Posts — {{ block.super }}{% endblock %}

{% block content %}
  {% for post in posts %}
    <article>
      <h2><a href="{{ post.get_absolute_url }}">{{ post.title }}</a></h2>
      <p>By {{ post.author.get_full_name }} · {{ post.created_at|date:"M d, Y" }}</p>
      <p>{{ post.content|truncatewords:30 }}</p>
    </article>
  {% empty %}
    <p>No posts yet.</p>
  {% endfor %}

  {% if is_paginated %}
    {% include 'partials/_pagination.html' %}
  {% endif %}
{% endblock %}
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
**DTL Syntax Summary**

| Syntax | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `{{ var }}` | Output variable |
| `{{ var\|filter }}` | Apply filter |
| `{% tag %}` | Logic / control |
| `{# comment #}` | Template comment |

**Common filters:**
```
{{ name|upper }}          → ALICE
{{ date|date:"Y-m-d" }}   → 2025-01-15
{{ text|truncatewords:20 }}
{{ price|floatformat:2 }} → 9.99
{{ html|safe }}           → unescaped HTML
{{ list|join:", " }}      → a, b, c
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Template Inheritance {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    BASE["base.html<br/>{% block title %}<br/>{% block content %}<br/>{% block footer %}"]
    BASE --> BLOG_BASE["blog/base.html<br/>{% extends 'base.html' %}<br/>{% block content %}<br/>  nav + sidebar<br/>{% endblock %}"]
    BASE --> AUTH_BASE["auth/base.html<br/>{% extends 'base.html' %}<br/>{% block content %}<br/>  centered card<br/>{% endblock %}"]
    BLOG_BASE --> INDEX["blog/index.html<br/>{% extends 'blog/base.html' %}<br/>{% block content %}<br/>  post list<br/>{% endblock %}"]
    BLOG_BASE --> DETAIL["blog/detail.html<br/>{% extends 'blog/base.html' %}<br/>{% block content %}<br/>  post detail<br/>{% endblock %}"]
    AUTH_BASE --> LOGIN["auth/login.html<br/>{% extends 'auth/base.html' %}"]
    AUTH_BASE --> REGISTER["auth/register.html<br/>{% extends 'auth/base.html' %}"]
    PARTIAL(["_nav.html<br/>_pagination.html<br/>_flash.html<br/>(partials via include)"]) -.->|"{% include %}"| BLOG_BASE

    classDef base     fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef mid      fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef leaf     fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef partial  fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#aaa,color:#aaa,stroke-dasharray:4
    class BASE base
    class BLOG_BASE,AUTH_BASE mid
    class INDEX,DETAIL,LOGIN,REGISTER leaf
    class PARTIAL partial
```

---

## Forms {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**`forms.py`**
```python
from django import forms
from .models import Post

class PostForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model   = Post
        fields  = ['title', 'slug', 'category', 'content', 'status']
        widgets = {
            'content': forms.Textarea(attrs={'rows': 8}),
            'slug':    forms.TextInput(attrs={'placeholder': 'auto-generated'}),
        }
        labels  = {'status': 'Publication Status'}

    def clean_title(self):
        title = self.cleaned_data['title']
        if len(title) < 5:
            raise forms.ValidationError("Title must be at least 5 characters.")
        return title.strip()

    def clean(self):
        cleaned = super().clean()
        title   = cleaned.get('title', '')
        slug    = cleaned.get('slug', '')
        if title and not slug:
            from django.utils.text import slugify
            cleaned['slug'] = slugify(title)
        return cleaned
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
flowchart TD
    POST["POST data /<br/>files"] --> BIND["Bind to Form<br/>form = PostForm(request.POST)"]
    BIND --> VALID["form.is_valid()"]
    VALID -->|calls| FIELD["Field.clean()<br/>(type coercion<br/>+ field validators)"]
    FIELD --> FORM_FIELD["clean_&lt;fieldname&gt;()<br/>(per-field custom logic)"]
    FORM_FIELD --> CLEAN["clean()<br/>(cross-field validation)"]
    CLEAN -->|pass| DATA["form.cleaned_data<br/>(safe Python values)"]
    CLEAN -->|fail| ERRORS["form.errors<br/>(render back to user)"]
    DATA --> SAVE["form.save()<br/>→ Model instance"]

    classDef input  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef step   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef output fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    classDef err    fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class POST,BIND input
    class VALID,FIELD,FORM_FIELD,CLEAN step
    class DATA,SAVE output
    class ERRORS err
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Django Admin {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# blog/admin.py
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Post, Category, Tag

@admin.register(Post)
class PostAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display   = ['title', 'author', 'status', 'created_at']
    list_filter    = ['status', 'category', 'created_at']
    search_fields  = ['title', 'content', 'author__username']
    prepopulated_fields = {'slug': ('title',)}
    raw_id_fields  = ['author']
    date_hierarchy = 'created_at'
    ordering       = ['-created_at']
    readonly_fields = ['created_at', 'updated_at']

    fieldsets = (
        ('Content', {
            'fields': ('title', 'slug', 'content', 'category', 'tags')
        }),
        ('Meta', {
            'fields': ('author', 'status', 'created_at', 'updated_at'),
            'classes': ('collapse',),
        }),
    )

    actions = ['make_published']

    def make_published(self, request, queryset):
        queryset.update(status='published')
    make_published.short_description = "Mark selected posts as published"

admin.site.register(Category)
admin.site.register(Tag)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    ADMIN["Django Admin Site<br/>/admin/"] --> LIST["Change List View<br/>list_display<br/>list_filter<br/>search_fields"]
    ADMIN --> DETAIL["Change Form View<br/>fieldsets<br/>prepopulated_fields<br/>readonly_fields"]
    ADMIN --> ACTIONS["Bulk Actions<br/>delete<br/>custom actions"]
    LIST --> INLINE["Inline Models<br/>TabularInline<br/>StackedInline"]
    DETAIL --> INLINE

    classDef root  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef node  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class ADMIN root
    class LIST,DETAIL,ACTIONS,INLINE node
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Setup:**
```bash
python manage.py createsuperuser
# → visit /admin/
```
Admin auto-generates full **CRUD** for every registered model.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Authentication & Authorization {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# Using built-in auth views (urls.py)
from django.contrib.auth import views as auth_views

urlpatterns = [
    path('login/',    auth_views.LoginView.as_view(),  name='login'),
    path('logout/',   auth_views.LogoutView.as_view(), name='logout'),
    path('password/', auth_views.PasswordChangeView.as_view()),
]

# Protecting views
from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required, permission_required
from django.contrib.auth.mixins import LoginRequiredMixin, PermissionRequiredMixin

@login_required
def dashboard(request):
    return render(request, 'dashboard.html')

@permission_required('blog.add_post', raise_exception=True)
def create_post(request): ...

class PostCreateView(LoginRequiredMixin, CreateView):
    login_url = '/login/'
    model     = Post
    fields    = ['title', 'content']
```

::: {.fragment}
```python
# Custom User model (do this early!)
# models.py
from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractUser

class CustomUser(AbstractUser):
    bio    = models.TextField(blank=True)
    avatar = models.ImageField(upload_to='avatars/', blank=True)

# settings.py
AUTH_USER_MODEL = 'accounts.CustomUser'
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    USER["User"] -->|belongs to| GROUPS["Groups"]
    GROUPS -->|has| PERMS["Permissions<br/>(app.action_model)"]
    USER -->|direct| PERMS
    PERMS -->|checked by| DECORATOR["@login_required<br/>@permission_required<br/>LoginRequiredMixin"]
    DECORATOR -->|controls| VIEW["View Access"]

    classDef user fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef perm fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef ctrl fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class USER user
    class GROUPS,PERMS perm
    class DECORATOR,VIEW ctrl
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Built-in permissions per model:**  
`blog.add_post`  
`blog.view_post`  
`blog.change_post`  
`blog.delete_post`
:::
:::
:::

---

## Middleware {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
Middleware is a **hook into Django's request/response processing** — a lightweight plugin system.

```python
# settings.py
MIDDLEWARE = [
    'django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.messages.middleware.MessageMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.clickjacking.XFrameOptionsMiddleware',
]
```

::: {.fragment}
**Custom middleware:**
```python
class TimingMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response

    def __call__(self, request):
        import time
        start = time.time()
        response = self.get_response(request)
        duration = time.time() - start
        response['X-Duration-Ms'] = int(duration * 1000)
        return response
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    REQ["Incoming Request"] --> MW1["SecurityMiddleware<br/>process_request ↓"]
    MW1 --> MW2["SessionMiddleware<br/>process_request ↓"]
    MW2 --> MW3["AuthMiddleware<br/>process_request ↓"]
    MW3 --> VIEW["View Function"]
    VIEW --> MW3R["AuthMiddleware<br/>↑ process_response"]
    MW3R --> MW2R["SessionMiddleware<br/>↑ process_response"]
    MW2R --> MW1R["SecurityMiddleware<br/>↑ process_response"]
    MW1R --> RES["Outgoing Response"]

    classDef req  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef mw   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef view fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef res  fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class REQ req
    class MW1,MW2,MW3,MW1R,MW2R,MW3R mw
    class VIEW view
    class RES res
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Signals {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
Signals allow **decoupled components** to react to events elsewhere in the application.

```python
from django.db.models.signals import post_save, pre_delete
from django.contrib.auth.signals import user_logged_in
from django.dispatch import receiver
from .models import Post

# Auto-create a user profile when a new user is created
@receiver(post_save, sender=User)
def create_profile(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):
    if created:
        Profile.objects.create(user=instance)

# Send notification when a post is published
@receiver(post_save, sender=Post)
def notify_on_publish(sender, instance, **kwargs):
    if instance.status == 'published':
        send_notification(instance.author, instance)

# Log deletions
@receiver(pre_delete, sender=Post)
def log_post_deletion(sender, instance, **kwargs):
    AuditLog.objects.create(
        action='delete',
        model='Post',
        object_id=instance.pk,
    )
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    SENDER["Sender<br/>(Model / View)"] -->|fires| SIGNAL["Signal<br/>(post_save<br/>pre_delete<br/>request_started...)"]
    SIGNAL -->|dispatches to| R1["Receiver 1<br/>create_profile()"]
    SIGNAL -->|dispatches to| R2["Receiver 2<br/>notify_on_publish()"]
    SIGNAL -->|dispatches to| R3["Receiver 3<br/>log_deletion()"]

    classDef sender fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef sig    fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef recv   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class SENDER sender
    class SIGNAL sig
    class R1,R2,R3 recv
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Built-in signals:**

- `pre_save` / `post_save`
- `pre_delete` / `post_delete`
- `m2m_changed`
- `request_started` / `request_finished`
- `user_logged_in` / `user_logged_out`
:::
:::
:::

---

## Caching {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# settings.py — Redis cache backend
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': 'redis://127.0.0.1:6379/1',
    }
}

# Per-view caching (decorator)
from django.views.decorators.cache import cache_page

@cache_page(60 * 15)   # cache for 15 minutes
def post_list(request):
    ...

# Low-level cache API
from django.core.cache import cache

def get_popular_posts():
    key = 'popular_posts'
    posts = cache.get(key)
    if posts is None:
        posts = list(
            Post.objects.annotate(views=Count('view'))
                        .order_by('-views')[:10]
        )
        cache.set(key, posts, timeout=300)
    return posts

# Template fragment caching
# {% load cache %}
# {% cache 300 sidebar user.id %}
#   ... expensive sidebar ...
# {% endcache %}
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    REQ["Request"] --> CHECK{"Cache hit?"}
    CHECK -->|HIT| CACHED["Return cached<br/>response instantly"]
    CHECK -->|MISS| VIEW["Execute View<br/>+ DB queries"]
    VIEW --> STORE["Store in cache<br/>(Redis / Memcached)"]
    STORE --> RES["Return response"]
    CACHED --> RES

    classDef req   fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef check fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef hit   fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    classDef miss  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class REQ req
    class CHECK check
    class CACHED,RES hit
    class VIEW,STORE miss
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Cache levels:**
- **Per-site** — entire site cached
- **Per-view** — `@cache_page`
- **Template fragment** — `{% cache %}`
- **Low-level API** — `cache.get/set`
:::
:::
:::

---

## Django REST Framework (DRF) {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# pip install djangorestframework
# INSTALLED_APPS: 'rest_framework'

from rest_framework import serializers, viewsets, routers
from rest_framework.permissions import IsAuthenticatedOrReadOnly

class PostSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    author = serializers.StringRelatedField()

    class Meta:
        model  = Post
        fields = ['id', 'title', 'slug', 'author',
                  'content', 'status', 'created_at']
        read_only_fields = ['created_at']

class PostViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):
    queryset           = Post.objects.all().order_by('-created_at')
    serializer_class   = PostSerializer
    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticatedOrReadOnly]

    def perform_create(self, serializer):
        serializer.save(author=self.request.user)

# urls.py
router = routers.DefaultRouter()
router.register(r'posts', PostViewSet)

urlpatterns = [
    path('api/', include(router.urls)),
    path('api-auth/', include('rest_framework.urls')),
]
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    CLIENT["Client<br/>(Browser / App)"] -->|HTTP JSON| ROUTER["DRF Router<br/>/api/posts/<br/>/api/posts/1/"]
    ROUTER --> VS["ViewSet<br/>list / create<br/>retrieve / update<br/>destroy"]
    VS --> PERM["Permission Classes<br/>IsAuthenticated<br/>IsAdminUser"]
    VS --> THROTTLE["Throttle Classes<br/>rate limiting"]
    VS --> SER["Serializer<br/>validate + serialize<br/>deserialize"]
    SER --> MODEL["Model / ORM"]
    MODEL --> SER
    SER --> VS
    VS --> CLIENT

    classDef client fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef drf    fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef layer  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef model  fill:#16213e,stroke:#aaa,color:#ccc
    class CLIENT client
    class ROUTER,VS drf
    class PERM,THROTTLE,SER layer
    class MODEL model
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Auto-generated endpoints:**  
`GET /api/posts/` — list  
`POST /api/posts/` — create  
`GET /api/posts/1/` — retrieve  
`PUT /api/posts/1/` — update  
`PATCH /api/posts/1/` — partial update  
`DELETE /api/posts/1/` — destroy  
:::
:::
:::

---

## Testing {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from django.test import TestCase, Client
from django.urls import reverse
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from .models import Post, Category

class PostModelTest(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.user = User.objects.create_user(
            username='alice', password='pass'
        )
        self.cat = Category.objects.create(name='Tech', slug='tech')

    def test_post_creation(self):
        post = Post.objects.create(
            title='Test Post', slug='test-post',
            author=self.user, category=self.cat,
            content='Hello world', status='published'
        )
        self.assertEqual(str(post), 'Test Post')
        self.assertEqual(Post.objects.count(), 1)

class PostViewTest(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.client = Client()
        self.user = User.objects.create_user('bob', password='pass')

    def test_index_view(self):
        res = self.client.get(reverse('blog:index'))
        self.assertEqual(res.status_code, 200)
        self.assertTemplateUsed(res, 'blog/index.html')

    def test_create_requires_login(self):
        res = self.client.get(reverse('blog:create'))
        self.assertRedirects(res, '/login/?next=/blog/new/')
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    SUITE["Test Suite<br/>python manage.py test"] --> UNIT["Unit Tests<br/>TestCase<br/>(models, forms,<br/>utils)"]
    SUITE --> INT["Integration Tests<br/>Client()<br/>(views, URLs,<br/>templates)"]
    SUITE --> API["API Tests<br/>APIClient()<br/>(DRF endpoints)"]

    UNIT --> ASSERT["Assertions<br/>assertEqual<br/>assertContains<br/>assertRaises<br/>assertTemplateUsed"]
    INT  --> ASSERT
    API  --> ASSERT

    classDef root   fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef type   fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef assert fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class SUITE root
    class UNIT,INT,API type
    class ASSERT assert
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
```bash
python manage.py test             # all tests
python manage.py test blog        # app only
python manage.py test blog.tests.PostModelTest
coverage run manage.py test && coverage report
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Deployment Architecture {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'flowchart': {'curve': 'orthogonal'}}}%%
graph TD
    USER["🌐 Users"] -->|HTTPS| CDN["CDN<br/>(CloudFront / Cloudflare)<br/>Static + Media files"]
    USER -->|HTTPS| LB["Load Balancer<br/>(Nginx / AWS ALB)"]
    LB  -->|HTTP| GUNICORN["Gunicorn / uWSGI<br/>(WSGI/ASGI Workers)"]
    GUNICORN -->|WSGI| DJANGO["Django Application"]
    DJANGO  -->|ORM| DB["PostgreSQL<br/>(Primary)"]
    DB      -->|replication| DBREP["PostgreSQL Replica<br/>(Read-only)"]
    DJANGO  -->|cache| REDIS["Redis<br/>(Cache + Sessions)"]
    DJANGO  -->|queue| CELERY["Celery Workers<br/>(Async Tasks)"]
    CELERY  -->|broker| REDIS
    DJANGO  -->|store| S3["Object Storage<br/>(S3 / GCS)<br/>Media files"]
    CDN     -.-> S3

    classDef user    fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef infra   fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef app     fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef data    fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class USER user
    class CDN,LB,GUNICORN infra
    class DJANGO,CELERY app
    class DB,DBREP,REDIS,S3 data
```

---

## Production Checklist {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Security**

- [ ] `DEBUG = False`
- [ ] `SECRET_KEY` from env variable
- [ ] `ALLOWED_HOSTS` configured
- [ ] `SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True`
- [ ] `CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True`
- [ ] `SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True`
- [ ] `SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 31536000`
- [ ] `X_FRAME_OPTIONS = 'DENY'`

**Database**

- [ ] PostgreSQL (not SQLite)
- [ ] DB credentials from env variables
- [ ] Connection pooling (pgBouncer)
- [ ] Regular backups scheduled
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Performance**

- [ ] Caching backend configured (Redis)
- [ ] Static files — `collectstatic` + CDN
- [ ] Media files — S3 / GCS
- [ ] `select_related` / `prefetch_related` used
- [ ] Database indexes on filtered fields

**Operations**

- [ ] `gunicorn` or `uvicorn` as WSGI/ASGI server
- [ ] Nginx as reverse proxy
- [ ] Environment variables via `.env` / secrets manager
- [ ] Logging configured (`LOGGING` setting)
- [ ] Health-check endpoint
- [ ] Celery for async tasks
- [ ] Monitoring (Sentry, Datadog)
:::
:::

---

## Django Ecosystem {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
mindmap
  root((Django<br/>Ecosystem))
    API
      djangorestframework
      django-ninja
      drf-spectacular
    Auth
      django-allauth
      social-auth-app-django
      dj-rest-auth
    Database
      django-environ
      django-extensions
      psycopg2
    Files & Storage
      django-storages
      Pillow
      django-imagekit
    Tasks & Scheduling
      celery
      django-celery-beat
      django-q
    Frontend
      django-htmx
      django-crispy-forms
      django-widget-tweaks
    Testing
      factory-boy
      pytest-django
      model-bakery
    Performance
      django-debug-toolbar
      django-silk
      whitenoise
    Deployment
      gunicorn
      uvicorn
      whitenoise
```

---

## Summary {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    subgraph REQUEST["Request Phase ▶"]
        MW1["Middleware<br/>(in)"] --> URL["URL Dispatcher"]
        URL --> VIEW["View"]
    end

    subgraph DATA["Data Layer"]
        VIEW --> ORM["ORM / Models"]
        ORM --> DB[(Database)]
        DB --> ORM
        ORM --> VIEW
        VIEW --> CACHE["Cache<br/>(Redis)"]
        CACHE --> VIEW
    end

    subgraph RESPONSE["◀ Response Phase"]
        VIEW --> FORM_VAL["Form Validation"]
        FORM_VAL --> TMPL["Template Engine"]
        TMPL --> MW2["Middleware<br/>(out)"]
        MW2 --> CLIENT["Client"]
    end

    subgraph ASYNC["Async"]
        VIEW --> SIGNAL["Signals"]
        SIGNAL --> CELERY["Celery Tasks"]
    end

    classDef req   fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef data  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef resp  fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef async fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
```

::: {.fragment}
**Key Takeaways:**

- MVT pattern separates concerns cleanly
- ORM abstracts database entirely — write Python, not SQL
- Admin panel is production-ready out-of-the-box
- Rich ecosystem — REST APIs, async tasks, caching, auth — all covered
- "Batteries included" philosophy accelerates development dramatically
:::
